seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pitt Town and Pitt Town Bottoms. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pitt Town vs Pitt Town Bottoms: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pitt Town and Pitt Town Bottoms.
heading: Pitt Town vs Pitt Town Bottoms — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pitt Town, New South Wales and Pitt Town Bottoms, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pitt Town has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Pitt Town Bottoms (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pitt Town or Pitt Town Bottoms safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pitt Town has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Pitt Town Bottoms scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pitt Town scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Pitt Town Bottoms scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pitt Town or Pitt Town Bottoms?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pitt Town has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
